name: ceremonies
description: Build the sprint ceremony infrastructure. This is the foundation of the file-based PM system that manages all subsequent phases. We are dogfooding: the tooling that runs Sovereign is built using the same tooling pattern that Sovereign PM will eventually provide via web UI.
sprintGoal: A working ceremonies.sh that can run a full agile cycle: refine → plan → execute → review → retro. After this phase, all subsequent phases are managed by ceremonies.sh, not manually.

stories.title: ralph.sh: --prd flag and manifest-aware active sprint resolution
stories.description: Modify scripts/ralph/ralph.sh to support a --prd <path> flag that overrides the hardcoded prd.json path. Default behaviour (no flag): read prd/manifest.json, find activeSprint field, use that file as the PRD. This makes ralph phase-aware without any other changes. Also update the default PRD_FILE fallback to prd/manifest.json resolution so existing `./ralph.sh` calls work unchanged if manifest.json exists. Add --phase <N> shorthand: resolves to the file field of phases[N] in manifest.json. Preserve all existing behaviour if neither flag is passed and no manifest.json exists (backward compat: fall back to scripts/ralph/prd.json).

smart.notes: Tiny, well-scoped bash change. Clear inputs and outputs.

stories.testPlan: Run ./ralph.sh --prd prd/phase-0-ceremonies.json and verify it uses that file. Run ./ralph.sh --phase 0 and verify it resolves from manifest.json. Run ./ralph.sh with no flags and verify it falls back correctly. shellcheck must pass.
acceptanceCriteria: --prd <path> flag overrides the PRD file path
--phase <N> resolves to phases[N].file from prd/manifest.json
Default with no flags: reads prd/manifest.json activeSprint if manifest exists
Backward compat: falls back to scripts/ralph/prd.json if no manifest
shellcheck passes on modified ralph.sh
Existing ralph.sh tests (if any) still pass

stories.title: prd/ directory: manifest schema, story schema v2, backlog migration, advance.sh
stories.description: Formalise the prd/ directory structure. (1) Write prd/schema/story.schema.json — JSON Schema for a story object with all v2 fields: id, phase, priority, points, branchName, title, description, smart{specific,measurable,achievable,relevant,timeBound,notes}, testPlan, dependencies[], acceptanceCriteria[], passes, reviewed, reviewNotes[], attempts. (2) Write prd/schema/manifest.schema.json — JSON Schema for manifest.json. (3) Migrate the monolithic root prd.json to prd/backlog.json. During migration: fix story 009 (rename charts/vault → charts/openbao, switch to openbao/openbao chart — BSL blocked); fix story 019 and 022 to remove all Bazel references (replace with ko/docker); add points estimates, testPlan, and dependency fields to every story; mark stories 001-011 as reviewed:false (they passed Ralph's self-grade but need independent review). (4) Write prd/advance.sh: marks current phase complete in manifest.json (sets status:complete, endDate, pointsCompleted, reviewPassRate), increments currentPhase, sets next phase status:active and activeSprint to next phase file. Guards: must not advance if any story in active sprint has passes:false or reviewed:false.

smart.notes: Achievable score 4: migration of 36 stories with new fields is mechanical but verbose. Acceptable in one story given the points budget.

stories.testPlan: Validate prd/backlog.json against story.schema.json using ajv-cli. Validate prd/manifest.json against manifest.schema.json. Check story 009 references openbao. Check stories 019/022 have no Bazel references. Run prd/advance.sh --dry-run and verify output. shellcheck advance.sh.
acceptanceCriteria: prd/schema/story.schema.json defines all v2 story fields
prd/schema/manifest.schema.json defines manifest structure
prd/backlog.json passes JSON Schema validation
Story 009 in backlog.json references openbao/openbao (not hashicorp/vault)
Stories 019 and 022 in backlog.json have no Bazel references
Every story in backlog.json has points, testPlan, dependencies fields
prd/advance.sh marks phase complete and advances manifest.json
prd/advance.sh refuses to advance if any story has passes:false or reviewed:false
prd/advance.sh --dry-run prints what would change without modifying files
shellcheck passes on advance.sh

stories.title: ceremonies/refine.md and ceremonies/smart-check.md: backlog health prompts
stories.description: Write two ceremony prompt files used before sprint planning. (1) scripts/ralph/ceremonies/refine.md: instructs Claude to read prd/backlog.json, identify stories that are not ready (vague descriptions, missing ACs, no testPlan, dependencies not listed, title not action-oriented), output a structured refinement report as prd/refinement-report.json with fields: storyId, issue, suggestion. For stories that are too large (points > 3), propose a split into two stories and write them into prd/proposed-splits.json. Does NOT modify backlog.json directly — human reviews proposals first. (2) scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart-check.md: instructs Claude to score every story in the active sprint file against SMART dimensions (1-5 each), write scores into smart{} fields in the sprint file, flag any story with a dimension score < 3 as not-ready and write a specific improvement note into smart.notes. A story is sprint-ready if all SMART dimensions >= 3. Output a summary table to stdout.

smart.notes: Prompt files — well scoped, clear output formats defined.

stories.testPlan: Run the refine ceremony against prd/backlog.json (with a deliberately vague test story added). Verify prd/refinement-report.json is produced with correct structure. Run smart-check against prd/phase-0-ceremonies.json and verify smart{} fields are populated on all stories. Verify any story with a dimension < 3 is flagged.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ralph/ceremonies/refine.md exists and instructs Claude to read backlog.json
refine.md produces prd/refinement-report.json with storyId, issue, suggestion per finding
refine.md produces prd/proposed-splits.json for stories with points > 3
refine.md does NOT modify backlog.json directly
scripts/ralph/ceremonies/smart-check.md exists
smart-check.md writes smart{} scores into the active sprint file
smart-check.md flags stories with any dimension < 3 in smart.notes
smart-check.md outputs a summary table to stdout

stories.title: ceremonies/plan.md: sprint planning — pull from backlog, point stories, check readiness
stories.description: Write scripts/ralph/ceremonies/plan.md. This ceremony runs at the start of a new sprint to populate a phase file from the backlog. Instructions to Claude: (1) Read prd/manifest.json to find the next pending phase. (2) Read prd/backlog.json and find stories for that phase (by phase field), ordered by priority. (3) For each candidate story, estimate story points (1=trivial config, 2=single chart or script, 3=complex multi-file feature, 5=must-split) and write to points field. (4) Check definition-of-ready: description is specific, all ACs are verifiable (have a concrete pass/fail check), testPlan exists, dependencies are listed and exist in backlog or a completed sprint. Mark not-ready stories with a readinessNote. (5) Pull stories into the phase file respecting the sprint capacity from manifest phases[N].capacity (default 15 points). Leave not-ready stories in backlog with readinessNote. (6) Write the new phase file. (7) Update manifest.json: set phase status to active, set activeSprint. Output a sprint planning summary: X stories pulled, Y points committed, Z stories left in backlog with reasons.

smart.notes: Clear input/output contract. Output format well defined.

stories.testPlan: Run plan ceremony targeting phase-1. Verify a phase-1-bootstrap.json is created with stories totalling <= 15 points. Verify not-ready stories remain in backlog.json with readinessNote. Verify manifest.json updated with activeSprint. Check JSON Schema validation passes on the produced phase file.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ralph/ceremonies/plan.md exists
plan.md reads manifest.json to find next pending phase
plan.md pulls stories from backlog.json by phase field ordered by priority
plan.md assigns points to every pulled story
plan.md respects sprint capacity (total points <= capacity)
plan.md leaves not-ready stories in backlog with readinessNote
plan.md creates the phase file at the path defined in manifest phases[N].file
plan.md updates manifest.json activeSprint and phase status
Produced phase file passes prd/schema/story.schema.json validation
Sprint planning summary printed to stdout

stories.title: ceremonies/review.md: independent AC verification — the definition of done
stories.description: Write scripts/ralph/ceremonies/review.md. This is the most critical ceremony: it independently verifies that stories Ralph marked passes:true actually satisfy their acceptanceCriteria. Ralph self-grades; this ceremony is the independent second opinion. Instructions to Claude: (1) Read the active sprint file. (2) For each story where passes:true and reviewed:false: re-read acceptanceCriteria. For each criterion, run the appropriate verification: 'helm lint passes' → run helm lint and capture exit code; 'shellcheck passes' → run shellcheck; 'file X exists' → check existence; 'values.yaml contains Y' → grep/parse; 'replicaCount >= 2' → parse YAML and assert; 'kubectl dry-run passes' → run it; 'npm run typecheck passes' → run it. (3) If ALL criteria verified: set reviewed:true. (4) If any criterion fails: set passes:false, append specific failure detail to reviewNotes[], increment attempts. (5) After reviewing all stories, output a review report: X/Y stories accepted, list any failures with exact criterion text and failure detail. (6) If any stories were re-opened (passes set back to false), write 'STORIES_REOPENED=true' to a temp file so ceremonies.sh knows to re-run ralph. The review ceremony must itself be idempotent: safe to run multiple times.

smart.notes: Achievable 4: running actual quality gates requires the right tools installed. Prompt must gracefully handle missing tools (kubectl, helm, etc.) by noting the gap rather than erroring out.

stories.testPlan: Create a test story with passes:true but a deliberately failing AC (a file that does not exist). Run review ceremony and verify: story gets passes:false, reviewNotes contains specific failure, reviewed remains false. Then fix the file, re-run review, verify reviewed:true. Verify STORIES_REOPENED is written correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ralph/ceremonies/review.md exists
review.md runs helm lint for 'helm lint passes' criteria
review.md runs shellcheck for 'shellcheck passes' criteria
review.md checks file existence for 'file X exists' criteria
review.md parses YAML for value assertions
review.md sets reviewed:true only when ALL criteria pass
review.md sets passes:false and appends to reviewNotes when any criterion fails
review.md increments attempts on each re-open
review.md writes STORIES_REOPENED signal for ceremonies.sh
review.md is idempotent (safe to run multiple times)
Review report printed to stdout with pass/fail per story

stories.title: ceremonies/retro.md: retrospective — extract learnings, update CLAUDE.md, track velocity
stories.description: Write scripts/ralph/ceremonies/retro.md. Runs after all stories in the active sprint are reviewed:true. Instructions to Claude: (1) Read the active sprint file and collect all reviewNotes from stories that had passes set back to false (stories that failed review at least once). (2) Read progress.txt entries since the last sprint start (use the sprint startDate from manifest.json to filter). (3) Identify failure patterns: which types of ACs are consistently failing? Which quality gates does Ralph miss? Which story types are over-pointed or under-pointed? (4) Generate specific, actionable CLAUDE.md updates: new entries for the LEARNINGS section, updated quality gate guidance, updated story patterns to avoid. Write these as a diff/patch — do NOT apply directly, write to prd/retro-patch-phaseN.md for human review before applying. (5) Update manifest.json: set phase status:complete, endDate, pointsCompleted, reviewPassRate (stories accepted on first review / total stories), append sprint summary to sprintHistory[]. (6) Generate velocity data point: {phase, pointsCompleted, storiesAccepted, reviewPassRate, date}. (7) Print retro summary: what went well (high review pass rate stories), what to improve (patterns from failures), velocity trend if >1 sprint completed.

smart.notes: Well defined. Output to prd/retro-patch rather than directly modifying CLAUDE.md is intentionally safe.

stories.testPlan: Run retro ceremony after phase-0 completes. Verify manifest.json has phase 0 status:complete with correct metrics. Verify prd/retro-patch-phase0.md exists with CLAUDE.md update suggestions. Verify sprintHistory has a new entry. Verify velocity[] has a new data point.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ralph/ceremonies/retro.md exists
retro.md reads reviewNotes from re-opened stories to identify failure patterns
retro.md reads progress.txt filtered by sprint start date
retro.md writes CLAUDE.md update suggestions to prd/retro-patch-phaseN.md (not applied directly)
retro.md sets phase status:complete in manifest.json with endDate and reviewPassRate
retro.md appends sprint summary to manifest.json sprintHistory[]
retro.md appends velocity data point to manifest.json velocity[]
retro.md prints retro summary to stdout
retro.md refuses to run if any story in the sprint still has reviewed:false

stories.title: ceremonies.sh: full sprint orchestrator with retry, review gates, and phase advancement
stories.description: Write scripts/ralph/ceremonies.sh — the single command that runs a complete sprint. Usage: ./ceremonies.sh [--phase N] [--tool claude|amp] [--max-retries 3]. Sequence: (1) SMART CHECK: run smart-check ceremony against the active sprint file. If any story scores < 3 on any SMART dimension, abort with instructions to refine those stories first. (2) EXECUTE: run ralph.sh --prd <activeSprint> --tool <tool> until all stories pass or max iterations hit. (3) REVIEW: run review ceremony. If STORIES_REOPENED signal detected, go back to EXECUTE (up to --max-retries times). If max retries hit with stories still failing: mark those stories with status:blocked, log details, continue with accepted stories. (4) RETRO: run retro ceremony. (5) ADVANCE: if all non-blocked stories reviewed:true, run prd/advance.sh to move to next phase. Print a clear summary at each step. Honour --dry-run flag: print what would happen without executing. Log all ceremony outputs to prd/logs/phase-N-<timestamp>.log for audit trail.

smart.notes: Achievable 4: orchestration logic with retry is moderately complex. Well within one story budget.

stories.testPlan: Run ./ceremonies.sh --dry-run --phase 0 and verify it prints the execution plan without modifying files. Run with a mock sprint file where one story will fail review and verify it retries ralph up to max-retries. Verify log file written to prd/logs/. shellcheck must pass.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ralph/ceremonies.sh exists and is executable
ceremonies.sh runs SMART check before execution and aborts on score < 3
ceremonies.sh runs ralph.sh for the active sprint
ceremonies.sh runs review ceremony after ralph completes
ceremonies.sh retries ralph if STORIES_REOPENED signal detected (up to --max-retries)
ceremonies.sh marks stories blocked after max retries exceeded
ceremonies.sh runs retro ceremony after review passes
ceremonies.sh runs advance.sh when all non-blocked stories reviewed:true
ceremonies.sh --dry-run prints plan without executing
ceremonies.sh logs all output to prd/logs/phase-N-<timestamp>.log
shellcheck passes on ceremonies.sh
